title: Validating HTML Headings with Strict Tag Names

statement.markdown:
Write a function that takes a string of HTML heading elements as input and returns an array of valid HTML heading tags. A valid heading tag is one that follows the strict syntax of HTML5, i.e., it must start with either `

`, `

`, `

`, `

`, `

`, or `
` and end with `
`, ``, ``, ``, ``, or ``. The function should ignore any tags that do not follow this strict syntax.
statement.inputFormat: A string of HTML heading elements, where each heading element is a string of characters enclosed in angle brackets (`<` and `>`) and separated by whitespace.
statement.outputFormat: An array of valid HTML heading tags, where each tag is a string of characters enclosed in angle brackets (`<` and `>`)
statement.notes: The function should be case-insensitive and ignore any whitespace characters between the angle brackets.

constraints.description: The number of heading elements in the input string

examples.explanation: The function should return an array containing the two valid heading tags.
The function should return an array containing the single valid heading tag.

hints.text: Start by splitting the input string into individual heading elements

editorial.title: Validating HTML Headings with Strict Tag Names
editorial.intuition: The function should use a regular expression to match the valid heading tags.
editorial.bruteForce: The function can use a simple loop to iterate over each heading element and check if it matches the valid syntax.
editorial.betterSolution: The function can use a regular expression to match the valid heading tags.
editorial.optimalSolution: The function can use a regular expression to match the valid heading tags and ignore any whitespace characters between the angle brackets.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The function should return an array containing only the valid heading tags.
